tropes.label: The Accidental Heir
tropes.summary: A later-born child inherits only because the firstborn falls — rebels, dies, or is disqualified. Power earned past a brother who was handed it.

tropes.label: Bootlegger to Legitimacy
tropes.summary: A vice/illicit fortune (bootlegging, smuggling, rackets) laundered across a generation into respectability, office, and old-money standing.

tropes.label: Frontier-Capital Origin
tropes.summary: The founding fortune is wrung from a colonial/extractive edge — emerald or gold or land on a frontier — then carried to the metropole.

tropes.label: Centrist Patriarch, Zealot Heir
tropes.summary: A deliberately apolitical/centrist founder builds broad legitimacy; the heir weaponizes it into a hard ideological or theocratic project.

tropes.label: The Conqueror
tropes.summary: Power taken and held by force; purges not elections; an order with no franchise, free press, or peaceful succession.

tropes.label: The Prophet / Theocrat
tropes.summary: Authority rooted in claimed divine mandate; the line rules souls as well as bodies, toward a religious-total state.

tropes.label: The Pleasure-King → Media Mogul
tropes.summary: A vice/entertainment empire (gambling, brothels, spectacle) laundered into media reach and propaganda legitimacy.

tropes.label: The Oligarch / Plutocrat
tropes.summary: Wealth fused with state power into a self-dealing plutocracy; markets and ministries serve the house.

tropes.label: The Techno-Frontiersman
tropes.summary: First-principles engineering ambition pushes the family to a literal or technological frontier — and toward techno-authoritarian temptation.

tropes.label: The Prosperity-Megachurch Builder
tropes.summary: A mass-media ministry monetized into an empire; faith as brand, congregation as market.

tropes.label: The Dissipating Line
tropes.summary: Inherited wealth squandered across generations — the rise's counterweight; heirs who spend, feud, and shrink the estate.

tropes.label: The Martyr
tropes.summary: An assassination or early death sanctifies the line — grief becomes dynastic capital, the fallen a permanent banner.

tropes.label: The Matriarch Regency
tropes.summary: A widow or mother holds and rebuilds the line through an interregnum — the keeper of the dynasty between patriarchs.

tropes.label: The Dynastic Merger
tropes.summary: Two houses fuse by marriage, combining fortunes and networks — the alliance that makes the next generation formidable.

tropes.label: The Cadet Branch
tropes.summary: A younger son founds a rival sub-dynasty; the line splits, and the branches compete or diverge across generations.

tropes.label: The Prodigal Heir
tropes.summary: The wild or rebel child who walks away — then returns to take the mantle, never quite forgiven, never quite secure.

tropes.label: The Scandal-Fall → Rehabilitation
tropes.summary: A public collapse — fraud, scandal, disgrace — followed by a calculated comeback that re-launders the name.

tropes.label: The Exile → Return
tropes.summary: A generation forced out — famine, pogrom, war, dispossession — whose descendants return, transformed, to reclaim a place.

tropes.label: The Reformer / Reactionary Schism
tropes.summary: A generation splits ideologically — one branch reforms, one entrenches — and the house's identity is contested from within.

tropes.label: The Outside Claimant
tropes.summary: A succession crisis from beyond the marriage — a bastard, a cousin, an adopted heir — destabilizes the line of descent.
